title: Swiggy Reimagined
subtitle: Streamlining India's Food Delivery: Reducing Cognitive Load, Enhancing Discovery.
problem_statement: Swiggy, while dominant, suffers from feature bloat, inconsistent UI patterns, and a lack of clear information hierarchy, leading to user frustration, decision paralysis, and increased cognitive load, ultimately impacting conversion and retention in a competitive market.

pillars.title: Decision Paralysis
pillars.insight: Users are overwhelmed by an excessive number of choices, promotions, and features, leading to analysis paralysis and abandonment.

pillars.title: Information Overload
pillars.insight: Key information such as delivery time, ratings, and offers is often inconsistently presented or buried, requiring excessive scanning and mental effort.

pillars.title: Inconsistent Experience
pillars.insight: UI elements and interaction patterns vary significantly across different service verticals (food, grocery, instamart), causing confusion and slowing down task completion.

artifacts.label: Strategy
artifacts.title: Defining the Challenges
questions: How might we simplify the user journey to reduce decision fatigue?
How might we improve information hierarchy to enhance discoverability and reduce cognitive load?
How might we create a more consistent and intuitive interface across all features?

points.name: Dunzo
points.desc: Simple, quick delivery, less personalized, focused on convenience.

solution.name: Swiggy (Redesign Target)
solution.desc: Balanced feature set with high personalization, intuitive simplicity, and reduced cognitive load.

personas.name: Arjun Sharma
personas.quote: I just want to order my usual quickly, but the app always tries to show me too much.
personas.bio: Arjun is a busy tech professional who values efficiency and speed. He often orders food after long work hours and prefers a straightforward, clutter-free experience. He gets frustrated by slow loading times and excessive promotional content that distracts from his primary goal.
needs: Clear visibility of delivery times and costs
Personalized recommendations based on past orders
frustrations: Too many irrelevant promotions on the home screen
Difficulty finding specific filters or sorting options

personas.name: Priya Singh
personas.quote: I love trying new places, but it's hard to discover them without getting lost in the app.
personas.bio: Priya is a social and adventurous young professional who enjoys exploring new restaurants and deals. She's price-sensitive and often looks for discounts. She uses Swiggy for both food and quick grocery runs but finds the app's complexity and lack of intuitive discovery features frustrating.
needs: Easy discovery of new restaurants and cuisines
Transparent display of offers and discounts
Seamless switching between food and grocery ordering
frustrations: Overwhelming number of categories and filters
Difficulty comparing options due to inconsistent information layout

quadrants.title: Says
items: "I just want to order quickly, why is this so complicated?"
"Is this the best deal, or am I missing something?"
"I wish it was easier to find new restaurants."

quadrants.title: Thinks
items: "I hope I didn't miss a better offer."
"Why does it take so long to load?"

quadrants.title: Does
items: Scrolls endlessly through the home screen.
Opens and closes multiple restaurant menus.
Abandons cart due to unexpected charges or confusion.
Switches between Swiggy and competitor apps to compare.

quadrants.title: Feels
items: Impatient with slow loading and complex flows.

points.action: Substitute the current cluttered home screen with an 'Intent-Driven' dashboard that dynamically adapts based on user's recent activity or time of day (e.g., 'Quick Bites', 'Dinner Plans', 'Grocery Run').
points.benefit: Reduces cognitive load by presenting relevant options upfront, minimizing decision paralysis.

points.action: Combine food, grocery, and other service searches into a single, intelligent universal search bar with predictive suggestions and clear service tags.
points.benefit: Enhances discoverability and user control, reducing the need to navigate separate sections and improving efficiency.

points.action: Adapt 'story' or 'reel' formats for daily deals, restaurant highlights, or new menu items, making discovery more engaging and less overwhelming than static banners.
points.benefit: Leverages familiar social media patterns for engaging content delivery, improving offer visibility and reducing banner blindness.

points.action: Modify the checkout flow into a single, clear, step-by-step process with a persistent cost breakdown, eliminating hidden charges and improving transparency.
points.benefit: Increases user trust and reduces cart abandonment by providing full cost visibility and a streamlined payment experience.

pivot.before: Swiggy's legacy friction stems from a 'more is more' approach, resulting in an overwhelming home screen, inconsistent navigation across services, and feature bloat that significantly increases user cognitive load and decision paralysis.
pivot.pivot_logic: The strategic pivot is to shift from a 'feature-heavy' to a 'curated simplicity' model. This involves prioritizing user intent, reducing decision points through intelligent personalization, establishing a unified design language, and ensuring transparent information hierarchy across all touchpoints.
pivot.after: The new vision for Swiggy is an intuitive, personalized, and efficient platform. This includes an intent-driven home screen, a unified and intelligent search experience, a streamlined and transparent checkout process, and a consistent design system that reduces cognitive load and enhances user satisfaction across all services.

stages.friction: Initial loading time, sometimes requires re-confirming location.

stages.touchpoints: Home screen, search bar, category chips
stages.actions: Scroll through feed, type in search, tap categories
stages.friction: Overwhelmed by promotions, too many options, inconsistent search results, difficulty filtering.

stages.actions: Tap on restaurant, scroll menu, add to cart
stages.friction: Unclear ratings/reviews, hidden delivery fees, complex menu navigation, difficulty comparing options.

stages.actions: Review order, apply coupon, select payment method, pay
stages.friction: Coupon application issues, unexpected charges appearing late, payment gateway delays, confusing address selection.
Inaccurate tracking, difficulty contacting delivery partner or customer support, generic error messages.

annotations.title: Unified Search Bar
annotations.rationale: Centralizes discovery for all Swiggy services, reducing cognitive load and improving efficiency by eliminating the need to switch contexts.

annotations.title: Intent-Driven 'For You' Feed
annotations.rationale: Dynamically curates relevant options based on user behavior and context, combating decision paralysis and enhancing personalization.

annotations.title: Persistent Cost Breakdown in Checkout
annotations.rationale: Increases transparency and builds trust by clearly showing all charges and discounts throughout the checkout process, preventing unexpected costs.

points: Implement a unified, intelligent search bar across all services.
Standardize offer display and application flow for clarity.
Improve loading indicators and system status feedback.
Establish a comprehensive design system for UI consistency.
Streamline the checkout process into a single, transparent flow.
Integrate AI-powered predictive recommendations and smart filters.
Refine error messages to be more helpful and actionable.
Optimize image loading and asset delivery for performance.
Introduce subtle haptic feedback for key interactions.

ideas.title: Intent-Driven Home Screen
ideas.desc: A dynamic home screen that intelligently adapts its layout and content based on user's immediate needs, past behavior, and time of day, significantly reducing cognitive load and enhancing relevant discovery.

ideas.title: Unified Smart Search & Filters
ideas.desc: A single, intelligent search bar that allows users to search across all Swiggy services (food, grocery, etc.) with natural language processing and highly relevant, consistent filtering options.

ideas.title: Transparent & Streamlined Checkout
ideas.desc: A simplified, multi-step checkout process with a persistent, clear breakdown of all costs (items, delivery, taxes, discounts) at every stage, eliminating hidden charges and building user trust.

ideas.title: Consistent Design Language System
ideas.desc: Implementation of a comprehensive design system to ensure visual and interaction consistency across all Swiggy verticals, reducing the learning curve and improving overall usability.

data.methodology: Remote moderated usability tests conducted with 10 target users (5 existing Swiggy users, 5 users of competitor apps) in India. Tasks included ordering a meal, finding a specific grocery item, and applying a discount code. Eye-tracking and think-aloud protocols were used.

findings.title: Offer Discovery Difficulty
findings.desc: Users consistently struggled to find and apply relevant discount codes, often missing out on available offers due to inconsistent placement and complex application flows.

findings.title: Cross-Service Confusion
findings.desc: Participants found it confusing to switch between food delivery and grocery (Instamart), often getting lost or accidentally navigating to the wrong service.

findings.title: Decision Fatigue on Home Screen
findings.desc: Many users expressed feeling overwhelmed by the sheer volume of restaurants, categories, and promotions on the home screen, leading to prolonged browsing or abandonment.

quotes: I just want to find something quick, but there's too much happening on the screen, I don't know where to look first.
I spent five minutes trying to find where to put the coupon code, it's never in the same place.
Is this for food or groceries? I keep tapping the wrong thing.
